name: VRChat Creator Error Corpus
notes: Seeded from real VRChat creator build, upload, Udon, sync, and workflow failures. Cases are advisory patterns, not automatic fixes.

cases.title: AssetBundle export assertion blocks world build
rawSignals: Assertion failed on expression: 'success && actual == size'
Error building Player: Failed to build scene
cases.rootCause: Unity failed while writing the VRChat world AssetBundle. The upload itself is not the first failure; the .vrcw bundle did not finish exporting.
cases.fixFirst: Close Unity, delete the project temp build folder, reopen Unity, optionally Reimport All, then run Build & Test before trying upload again.
ignoreUntilLater: TMP missing glyph warnings
Cosmetic console warnings unrelated to bundle export
vrchatSpecificNotes: VRChat upload cannot succeed if Unity never produces the world bundle.
If temp output has manifests but no real bundle, suspect interrupted/corrupt AssetBundle write.
recommendations: Clear the project temp folder under AppData/Local/Temp for the world.
Check antivirus/Defender or sync tools touching Unity temp files.
If it persists, isolate recently added assets, materials, audio, or scene objects.
regexSignals: Assertion failed on expression: 'success && actual == size'
Error building Player: Failed to build scene

cases.title: Existing world upload risk after failed builds
cases.rootCause: The project may be fixed locally, but direct upload risks replacing a live world before confirming the build loads.
cases.fixFirst: Run VRChat SDK Build & Test locally first. Only upload after the local client can join, spawn, and move around.
ignoreUntilLater: Minor performance polish after build errors are gone
vrchatSpecificNotes: When preserving visits/favorites, verify the SDK panel is still using the existing world blueprint ID.
A clean reupload is appropriate only after local Build & Test passes.
recommendations: Clear Console before Build & Test.
Confirm the target scene is the intended rebuild scene.
Restart VRChat after upload processing before testing the live world.

cases.title: Build & Test works but Build & Publish fails due to blueprint/account state
rawSignals: Build & Test works but Build & Publish fails
The world blueprint ID belongs to a different account or got into a bad SDK state
VRChat upload/API failure after the bundle is built
all fixed, it was pipelines blueprint id issue. detaching solved it
cases.rootCause: The Unity project can build, but the VRChat upload binding/account/blueprint state is invalid or stale. The issue is after bundle creation, not Udon compile.
cases.fixFirst: Log out/in through SDK Authentication, check VRChat website creator prompts, then detach or clear the Pipeline Manager blueprint ID only if preserving the old live world is not required for this upload path.
ignoreUntilLater: AmplitudeAPI analytics noise
Unrelated Unity warnings if local Build & Test succeeds
vrchatSpecificNotes: Do not delete the world from Content Manager just to fix a bad local blueprint binding.
Detaching Pipeline Manager uploads as a new world ID; it does not delete the old world.
If Private upload works but Public upload fails, suspect account/world permission state.
recommendations: Select the VRCWorld object and inspect Pipeline Manager.
Confirm the attached blueprint ID belongs to the logged-in account.
Disable VPN/proxy/antivirus web filtering for upload/API tests.
Use Private visibility once as an upload-path test.
regexSignals: Build & Test works but Build & Publish fails
VRChat upload/API failure after the bundle is built
blueprint ID belongs to a different account

cases.title: UdonSharp script compile error from broken RoleRegistrar
rawSignals: cannot find this file in my standard Assets folder view
cases.rootCause: A broken or hidden UdonSharp script blocks compilation. Unity may still reference scripts that are outside the visible folder view or stuck in cache/subfolders.
cases.fixFirst: Search the entire project directory for the script file, replace or repair it, then compile all UdonSharp programs.
ignoreUntilLater: Scene behavior bugs caused by the registrar until compile is clean
vrchatSpecificNotes: UdonSharp will not generate/update working Udon programs while C# compile errors exist.
A single broken .cs file can block unrelated Udon behaviours.
recommendations: Search outside the Unity Project window, including hidden/subfolders.
Prefer a minimal known-good UdonSharpBehaviour to unblock compile.
Run UdonSharp > Compile All UdonSharp Programs after repair.

cases.title: Unity sees UdonSharp .cs files but generated program assets are missing
rawSignals: Unity didnt generate asset files for them
cases.rootCause: Unity imported the raw scripts, but UdonSharp did not create or refresh the Program Source assets, often because compilation/import was blocked or an empty UdonBehaviour remains in the scene.
cases.fixFirst: Check the Editor log for UdonSharp import/compile errors, remove empty UdonBehaviour components, then force UdonSharp to regenerate program assets.
ignoreUntilLater: Inspector wiring for scripts whose Udon program assets do not exist yet
vrchatSpecificNotes: Raw .cs files are not enough; the scene needs usable UdonSharp program assets.
An Empty UdonBehaviour is a strong signal that a script/component reference is broken.
recommendations: Use an editor repair utility only for known missing Program Assets.
Remove or restore empty UdonBehaviour references.
Wait for Unity domain reload, then run Compile All UdonSharp Programs.

cases.title: 100 repeated build errors from stale UdonSharp program asset
cases.rootCause: Many console entries are repeats of one stale/broken UdonSharp behaviour or program asset. Odin serialization is failing because a Unity object reference inside the Udon program metadata is null.
cases.fixFirst: Use the Evidence/Suspect line to locate the broken UdonBehaviour or UdonSharp program asset. Remove or recreate that component/program source, then run UdonSharp > Compile All UdonSharp Programs.
ignoreUntilLater: Counting every repeated console entry as a separate bug
Canvas or renderer checkboxes if the stack trace points at UdonSharp/Odin
vrchatSpecificNotes: A scene-linked Udon program asset with no source script can break SDK build serialization.
The useful signal is the first full stack trace plus Unity context object, not the number of repeated errors.
recommendations: If a Suspect asset/object is shown, inspect that object first; it is the likely stale UdonBehaviour or program source.
If no Suspect is shown, open the first full Console error, select the referenced object if Unity highlights one, and rerun analysis.
Use UdonSharp > Compile All UdonSharp Programs after removing/recreating the broken component.
Project-specific repair tools are optional; regular users should not need one.

cases.title: Requested Networking.Instantiate API is not available in installed SDK
rawSignals: installed VRChat/UdonSharp SDK does not expose Networking.Instantiate
cases.rootCause: The proposed script used an API that this installed VRChat SDK does not expose, which would create a new compile blocker.
cases.fixFirst: Use the supported UdonSharp instantiate path for this SDK, typically Instantiate(...) compiled through VRChat's VRCInstantiate wrapper, and verify the prefab is valid for VRChat spawning.
ignoreUntilLater: Advanced pooled delivery logic until the API compiles
vrchatSpecificNotes: VRChat SDK API availability varies by installed SDK version.
Runtime spawned props can still behave local/client-sided without proper VRChat object setup.
recommendations: Search the installed SDK/examples before using an API from memory.
Prefer pre-placed object pools for reliable shared world props.
If spawning is required, ensure pickup/object sync setup is valid.

cases.title: Duplicated synced mirror triggers create separate realities
rawSignals: first joiner sees stage 3 events perfectly
first player sees both of them
two separate MirrorTrigger behaviours using the same stage objects
cases.rootCause: Two synced Udon behaviours controlled the same stage objects with separate synced state, so clients diverged depending on which trigger path they touched.
cases.fixFirst: Centralize breach start in one authority system and make each trigger ask that system to start all registered mirror triggers together.
ignoreUntilLater: Upload cache suspicion until duplicate scene behaviours are ruled out
vrchatSpecificNotes: Manual sync on multiple behaviours does not make them one shared state machine.
Duplicate Udon behaviours referencing the same objects are a classic late-join/current-player divergence risk.
recommendations: Search the scene for duplicate scripts of the same trigger type.
Use one synced authority for global state.
Refuse a second trigger while any registered trigger is already active.

cases.title: Non-owner clients repeatedly replay stage effects from deserialization
rawSignals: stage 3 alarms and VOX restart repeatedly
every client is advancing the synced stage locally
every deserialization can replay the transition side effects
added a per-client already applied this stage snapshot guard
cases.rootCause: Non-owner clients were both rendering and mutating/replaying stage transitions. Duplicate sync packets could restart audio, alarms, and timers.
cases.fixFirst: Make the stage owner-authoritative and idempotent: only the owner mutates currentStage, non-owners only apply received snapshots, and each client guards against replaying the same stage side effects.
ignoreUntilLater: Fine tuning stage timing before deserialization is idempotent
vrchatSpecificNotes: OnDeserialization may fire more than once for the same state.
Synced state application should be safe to repeat without restarting one-shot effects.
recommendations: Use manual sync for authoritative stage state.
Call RequestSerialization when the owner changes stage.
Track last applied stage locally to avoid repeating one-shot effects.

cases.title: Late joiners miss persistent stage objects
rawSignals: late joiner doesnt see any stages that are still in effect triggered before they join
stage 2 and stage 3 were transition steps, not full state snapshots
cases.rootCause: Stage application was coded as one-time transitions. Late joiners receiving the current stage only ran the latest transition and missed earlier persistent state.
cases.fixFirst: Make stage application cumulative. Applying stage 3 should also apply persistent stage 1 and stage 2 objects.
ignoreUntilLater: Fine-tuning effects timing before late-join state reconstruction works
vrchatSpecificNotes: OnDeserialization should reconstruct the full visible state, not just replay the latest transition.
Late-join compatibility requires state snapshots, not only event-like effects.
recommendations: Separate persistent state from one-shot audio/VFX.
Test by joining a second client after each stage is already active.
Keep stage state idempotent so repeated application does not restart everything.

cases.title: Timed synced sequence can freeze when owner leaves
rawSignals: if owner leaves during stage 1 or 2
timer that would advance the stage may vanish
stage 3 persists as serialized stage until reset
cases.rootCause: The current owner was responsible for delayed stage advancement. If that player left mid-sequence, the scheduled event could disappear with them.
cases.fixFirst: Handle ownership transfer by resuming the remaining timer when the new owner receives control.
ignoreUntilLater: Late stage polish until ownership transfer is robust
vrchatSpecificNotes: Object owner is not the same as instance master.
DelayedSendCustomEvent calls do not magically transfer when the owner leaves.
recommendations: Implement OnOwnershipTransferred or equivalent ownership-change handling.
Store enough synced state to resume progression.
Keep terminal reset routed through the owner-authoritative path.

cases.title: Networked interaction works but local sound is missing
rawSignals: all works now but there is no sound
door sounds now play locally immediately, then send the network sound event
cases.rootCause: The interaction path updated state, but audio relied too heavily on network-event timing or AudioSource settings, so the triggering player did not reliably hear feedback.
cases.fixFirst: Play local audio immediately for the triggering player, send a networked sound event for others, and add a short de-dupe guard.
ignoreUntilLater: Micro-polishing sound timing before AudioSource setup is verified
vrchatSpecificNotes: VRChat network events may not be the best source for immediate local feedback.
If VRChat echoes the event back, a small de-dupe timer prevents double playback.
recommendations: Verify AudioClip, Mute, Volume, Spatial Blend, Min Distance, Max Distance, and active GameObject.
Use local feedback first for the actor.
Use network event for observers.

cases.title: Role selection must update globally while feedback stays local
rawSignals: Player A clicks a role button
Player B observes the display text
Audio click plays locally for Player A
display text updates globally for all players synchronously
cases.rootCause: The interaction mixes two different requirements: global state for role display and local-only feedback for the clicking player.
cases.fixFirst: Separate role state from feedback. Sync the chosen role/display state, but play immediate local click feedback for the actor.
ignoreUntilLater: Fancy animation until state and feedback responsibilities are separated
vrchatSpecificNotes: Local feedback should not wait for network state to round-trip.
Observer displays should derive from synced state, not local-only UI changes.
recommendations: Use a synced role index or string-safe mapped int.
Apply display text in OnDeserialization.
Play click audio locally, then optionally notify observers.

cases.title: RequestSerialization without synced state does not sync collider toggle
rawSignals: Simply reading the updated network value
no synced bool in original code
cases.rootCause: The script requested serialization, but it had no [UdonSynced] field representing the collider state, so there was nothing meaningful to serialize.
cases.fixFirst: Store the collider enabled state in a [UdonSynced] bool, set ownership before toggling, call RequestSerialization, and apply the bool in OnDeserialization.
ignoreUntilLater: Complex ownership policy until the basic synced state exists
vrchatSpecificNotes: RequestSerialization only sends synced fields.
Late joiners need the synced bool to reconstruct the collider state.
recommendations: Add [UdonBehaviourSyncMode(BehaviourSyncMode.Manual)].
Add [UdonSynced] private bool _isColliderEnabled.
Apply the synced value both locally and in OnDeserialization.

cases.title: Door visuals move but collider state remains local or stale
rawSignals: elevator doors now show at same time, but collision box doesnt get disabled for the other client
other client clicking other button at other side disabled collision
visuals are moving, but authoritative object state/colliders are still not being driven from one agreed network source
cases.rootCause: Animation/visual state and collider/blocker state were not controlled by one authoritative synced state. Each button/controller could affect a different local blocker reference.
cases.fixFirst: Use one networked door state and apply visuals plus blocker colliders from that same state on every client.
ignoreUntilLater: Door polish until blockers and visuals agree across clients
vrchatSpecificNotes: A door that looks open but has a closed collider is worse than a failed animation.
Shared doors should not have per-side controllers managing separate blocker state.
recommendations: Support multiple blocker colliders if a door has multiple entrances.
Apply collider state continuously or from idempotent state while door is open/closed.
Test with two clients from both sides of the door.

cases.title: Runtime spawned props appear client-sided
rawSignals: coffee cup looks still client sided
spawning or toggling a coffee cup object is currently client-sided
players can only see their own cups
pre-placed pooled cup objects instead of runtime prefab spawning
cases.rootCause: The world used runtime prefab spawning or local toggles for props that needed to be shared, but VRChat worlds are more reliable with pre-placed pooled objects driven by synced state.
cases.fixFirst: Use disabled scene objects as a pool and activate/move them via synced state, or ensure any spawned prefab is set up for valid VRChat network spawning.
ignoreUntilLater: Fancy hand-delivery before shared visibility works
vrchatSpecificNotes: Normal Unity Instantiate is local-only in VRChat Udon contexts.
VRCInstantiate still requires the prefab to be valid for VRChat object sync/pickup behavior.
recommendations: Prefer pre-placed object pools for vending/coffee props.
Assign pooled objects in the dispenser inspector.
Set ownership when moving or activating shared pooled objects.

cases.title: Checkpoint buttons feel broken because spam prevention is too aggressive
rawSignals: security checkpoint lockdown and unlockdown buttons
you can only press them once now
cases.rootCause: The anti-spam/cooldown guard prevents legitimate repeated interactions, making the controls look broken even if the logic is technically firing.
cases.fixFirst: Reduce the cooldown and give visible/audio feedback when a press is ignored, so users understand the button state.
ignoreUntilLater: Complex permission model until the basic button cadence feels usable
vrchatSpecificNotes: In VRChat worlds, no feedback often reads as networking failure.
Cooldowns need to account for multiple users pressing shared controls.
recommendations: Use a short cooldown for shared lockdown/unlock controls.
Display current locked/unlocked state clearly.
Give denied/cooldown feedback instead of silently ignoring the press.

cases.title: Containment interaction needs clear timing feedback
cases.rootCause: The interaction needed a simple readable gameplay loop instead of a plain button press or overcomplicated minigame.
cases.fixFirst: Make the loop observe state, wait for the correct moment, then activate containment with obvious green/red feedback.
ignoreUntilLater: Complex minigame mechanics before the core timing affordance is readable
vrchatSpecificNotes: Visitors need to understand world interactions without reading external instructions.
Clear color/state feedback helps both onboarding and replayability.
recommendations: Use one clear timing window.
Show success/fail state directly on the terminal.
Keep failure recoverable unless it is intentionally part of the world event.

cases.title: Role UI changes but doors do not grant access
rawSignals: door is not running the updated FunctionalSlidingDoor Udon program yet
cases.rootCause: The visible registrar UI state changed, but the actual access grant path or updated door Udon program was not being invoked reliably.
cases.fixFirst: Make doors read a robust authority: player clearance tag first, then clearance system, then visible registrar display as fallback.
ignoreUntilLater: Cleaner architecture until the player can actually open the door
vrchatSpecificNotes: A UI display switching active does not prove an Udon event granted access.
If source code changed but behavior did not, verify UdonSharp recompiled and the scene object uses the updated program.
recommendations: Run UdonSharp > Compile All UdonSharp Programs.
Check the Button OnClick target invokes a concrete role method, not a stale generic Interact path.
Add observable fallbacks for debugging, but remove brittle fallbacks later.

cases.title: Door collision changes but animation and sound do not play
rawSignals: for the double door only collision works but there is no animation or sound
ForceOpen or ForceClose snapped the state without sound or animation
Door Colliders list contains the frame collider
cases.rootCause: The button path changed collision through a force/snap method or wrong collider reference, while the animation/audio path was bypassed or blocked by static/miswired door objects.
cases.fixFirst: Route ForceOpen/ForceClose through the same animated door action path, uncheck Static on moving door parts, and separate visible door meshes from invisible doorway blockers.
ignoreUntilLater: Fine tuning door timing before the action path drives animation, collision, and audio together
vrchatSpecificNotes: A VRChat interaction can appear to work if only the blocker changes, even when the visible object never moved.
Imported static props should not be used as moving door transforms.
recommendations: Use OpenDoor, CloseDoor, or ToggleDoor for animated movement.
Do not assign the frame collider as the moving door collider.
Check AudioSource clip, volume, spatial range, and active state.

cases.title: Door animation fails or snaps because imported parent scale distorts local offsets
rawSignals: local movement can behave like a giant jump
cases.rootCause: The moving door transform was under a heavily scaled imported parent, so local offsets were magnified or appeared to snap instead of animate naturally.
cases.fixFirst: Animate by fixed duration with smooth easing and world-space offsets, or place an unscaled hinge/driver object at the correct pivot.
ignoreUntilLater: Complex animation curves until transform scale/pivot is sane
vrchatSpecificNotes: Many imported environment meshes have large parent scale values.
World-space offsets are often safer for in-world utility scripts.
recommendations: Enable Use World Space Offset.
Use a separate invisible blocker collider.
For hinged doors, create an empty hinge object at the hinge edge and rotate that transform.

cases.title: World size and optimization uncertainty before upload
rawSignals: what is taking most space in my scene
cases.rootCause: The creator needs a prioritized size/performance breakdown before upload, not a generic optimization checklist.
cases.fixFirst: Rank largest assets and recent additions first, then distinguish upload-blocking size from polish-level optimization.
ignoreUntilLater: Small material or text tweaks if bundle size is dominated by a few large assets
vrchatSpecificNotes: World size concerns should be tied to upload readiness and user join/load experience.
A 75MB world may be acceptable or problematic depending on platform target and asset composition.
recommendations: List top textures, audio clips, meshes, and imported package folders by size.
Separate PC-only concerns from Quest constraints.
Run Build & Test after removing large assets to verify real bundle impact.

cases.title: Opening VRC SDK panel appears to resume or start upload
rawSignals: as soon as i opened vrc sdk, it started uploading itself
previous upload was already in progress/queued
Unity recompiled/reloaded, then the SDK continued the upload flow
cases.rootCause: The stock SDK should not upload merely from opening the panel. More likely, a previous upload flow resumed after reload or the SDK was showing a queued/in-progress publish state.
cases.fixFirst: Restart Unity, confirm the SDK panel is idle before pressing anything, then verify the active scene and website upload status before attempting another publish.
ignoreUntilLater: Blaming unrelated renderer checkboxes before checking SDK state and active scene
vrchatSpecificNotes: Unity domain reloads can make an SDK upload flow look like it resumed later.
Uploading the wrong active scene is a more serious risk than a harmless SDK panel refresh.
recommendations: Do not keep reuploading while diagnosis is unclear.
Check VRChat website/content manager for today's processed upload.
Use Build & Test to separate project-side failure from upload/cache/server-side failure.

cases.title: Random world event timers must run on one owner only
rawSignals: random one happening once every 15 mins to 45 mins
owner only, so you don't get every client rolling its own disaster clock
separate clients are running conflicting timers
cases.rootCause: Every client running its own random event timer creates conflicting world events, repeated announcements, and divergent state.
cases.fixFirst: Schedule random world events only on the authoritative owner and serialize the resulting state to everyone else.
ignoreUntilLater: Adding more random events before there is one authoritative event clock
vrchatSpecificNotes: VRChat instances need a single authority for shared timed world state.
Random timers should produce synced state, not independent local incidents.
recommendations: Check owner before scheduling random events.
Serialize the chosen event state/duration.
Make audio announcements idempotent so state refreshes do not restart loops.

cases.title: Generated textures look AI-made and reduce creator confidence
rawSignals: StabilizationConsoleTexture and anomObsConsoleTexture looks very ai
cases.rootCause: The assets communicated 'AI HUD noise' rather than intentional readable in-world UI.
cases.fixFirst: Replace decorative noise with aligned panels, restrained visual language, readable text, and no fake generated emblems.
ignoreUntilLater: More visual flair before readability and alignment pass
vrchatSpecificNotes: World props need to be readable at player distance and credible in context.
Texture layout matters for onboarding and first-time user comprehension.
recommendations: Use fewer ornaments and clearer hierarchy.
Preview the texture at in-world scale.
Avoid wrapping screen textures directly around cube sides; use a front-facing quad.

cases.title: Players need in-world changelog communication
rawSignals: make a changelog screen i can put at lobby
I wont guarantee to update to Discord anymore
cases.rootCause: Creator communication was fragmented between Discord and the world itself.
cases.fixFirst: Provide an in-world changelog prefab or generator that can be placed in the lobby and updated from one source.
ignoreUntilLater: External announcement polish before the in-world screen exists
vrchatSpecificNotes: For worlds, documentation and patch notes can be part of the playable space.
In-world changelogs help returning visitors understand why the world changed.
recommendations: Generate a reusable changelog prefab.
Keep copy concise enough to fit from normal lobby distance.
Update generator source and placed prefab together.

cases.title: Manual weekly backups leave a dangerous recovery gap

sourceThread.title: Archive terminal and project snapshot work

rawSignals: manually copy-pasting a backup folder once a week
local file structure collapsed between backup cycles
cases.rootCause: The creator lost confidence because recovery depended on sparse manual backups instead of frequent snapshots or version control.
cases.fixFirst: Automate project snapshots of Assets, Packages, and ProjectSettings while excluding Library.
ignoreUntilLater: Backing up Library or cache folders
vrchatSpecificNotes: VRChat world projects accumulate many scene/prefab/script changes quickly.
When a world becomes unjoinable, rollback granularity matters.
recommendations: Create snapshots on Unity quit and on manual command.
Keep the newest N snapshots.
Use Git or Unity Version Control when ready.

cases.title: Registrar console parent has a script but role button is unconfigured
rawSignals: Clicked security access but did not get level 2 access
cases.rootCause: The parent console object had a registrar component, but the actual configured grant behavior needed to live on the child role button. The clicked object could change visuals without granting clearance.
cases.fixFirst: Select the actual child role button, set the role type/name, assign ClearanceSystem, and enable local clearance updates.
ignoreUntilLater: Door tuning before verifying the role grant side works
vrchatSpecificNotes: Udon buttons often call behavior on a specific child object; a component on the visible parent is not enough.
Role grant and display state should be validated separately.
recommendations: Add inspector validation for missing clearance system references.
Show a local debug text with current role and clearance level during setup.
Prefer explicit child button names such as Button_Security.

cases.title: Stale serialized role label overrides the visible role button
rawSignals: Security display lights up but security door still refuses
cases.rootCause: The inspector kept an old serialized role string, so the object looked like a security button while its Udon data still resolved as another role.
cases.fixFirst: Reset the serialized role fields or make the script derive the role from an explicit enum/object name before using stale labels.
ignoreUntilLater: Rebuilding door logic before fixing the stale button data
vrchatSpecificNotes: Udon public variables can survive script changes and keep old values.
Visual display state is not proof that the underlying synced role value is correct.
recommendations: Prefer role enum/int fields over free text.
Add a one-line diagnostic log when a role button is pressed.
Use object-name fallback only as a setup safety net.

cases.title: Role buttons change display but never grant clearance
rawSignals: Player still cannot open restricted door
cases.rootCause: Existing scene objects did not have the new ClearanceSystem reference assigned, so RoleRegistrar returned before applying the local clearance.
cases.fixFirst: Assign the scene ClearanceSystem to every role registrar and restricted door, or add a safe runtime auto-resolve by object name.
ignoreUntilLater: Changing required door levels before the player's current clearance can be read
vrchatSpecificNotes: Adding a new public field to an UdonSharp script does not automatically fill existing scene references.
A visible Udon interaction can still fail silently if its target reference is null.
recommendations: Use a validation window to find null required references.
Add denied feedback that includes current clearance level during alpha testing.

cases.title: TMP UGUI field rejects world-space TextMeshPro object
rawSignals: status text doesnt accept game objects
cases.rootCause: The script exposed the UI-only TextMeshProUGUI type, but the object in the scene was a world-space TextMeshPro component.
cases.fixFirst: Change the field type to TMP_Text so both TextMeshPro and TextMeshProUGUI can be assigned.
ignoreUntilLater: Rebuilding the terminal UI before fixing the component type mismatch
vrchatSpecificNotes: VRChat worlds often mix canvas text and world-space TMP signs.
TMP_Text is the safer public field type for reusable UdonSharp UI scripts.
recommendations: Use TMP_Text for shared text output fields.
Name visible status text objects clearly, such as ContainmentHint.

cases.title: Script writes to hidden UI while the visible monitor never changes
rawSignals: I dont see anything on the screen during breach
cases.rootCause: The logic was updating a valid TMP component, but it was not the visible in-world monitor the creator was looking at.
cases.fixFirst: Rewire the status field to the visible text component and verify the object changes during Play mode.
ignoreUntilLater: Timing values and color polish before confirming the visible output target
vrchatSpecificNotes: Scene copies can preserve wrong UI references even when script logic is correct.
In-world monitor feedback should be tested from the player's expected viewpoint.
recommendations: Add a setup button that pings or flashes assigned UI targets.
Use unique names for visible and hidden TMP objects.

cases.title: Latejoiners see checkpoint gate open after lockdown
rawSignals: Latejoiners see the door in its default open state
Synchronization Method is Continuous or Manual
cases.rootCause: The button broadcasted an event to current players but did not store an authoritative synced gate state for players who joined later.
cases.fixFirst: Store the lockdown state in an UdonSynced bool, request serialization on state changes, and reapply state in Start and OnDeserialization.
ignoreUntilLater: Audio polish before persistent gate state works
vrchatSpecificNotes: Network events are not state snapshots.
Latejoiner-safe world state needs synced variables or another persistent state source.
recommendations: Use Manual sync for discrete gate states.
Keep lock and release on the same authoritative synced component when possible.

cases.title: Rapid checkpoint button spam restarts or stacks audio
rawSignals: Lock/unlock button can broadcast every click
cases.rootCause: The buttons sent effects even when the logical state did not change, causing repeated network events and audio retriggers.
cases.fixFirst: Guard state changes, ignore already-locked/already-unlocked presses, and add a short independent audio cooldown.
ignoreUntilLater: Changing siren clips before suppressing duplicate events
vrchatSpecificNotes: Networked audio spam can affect every player, not only the person pressing the button.
State no-op should usually mean no effect event.
recommendations: Separate interaction cooldown from audio cooldown.
Use local feedback for rejected spam if needed, not a global effect.

cases.title: Billiards shader dependency missing during world build
rawSignals: VRChat SDK wrapper error is not the actual cause
cases.rootCause: A material/shader from VRCBilliardsCE referenced LTCGI, but the LTCGI include file was not present in the project.
cases.fixFirst: Import the missing LTCGI dependency or replace/disable the Filamented LTCGI shader/material before building.
ignoreUntilLater: Role button warnings if the counted build errors are shader errors
vrchatSpecificNotes: VRChat SDK build failure dialogs often show only the wrapper; Editor.log has the real first error.
Third-party world assets can depend on optional shader packages.
recommendations: Search Editor.log for the first shader/compiler error above the SDK failure.
Keep dependency packages documented next to imported prefabs.

cases.title: Unity cannot write sharedAssets because the build disk is full
cases.rootCause: The VRChat build failed while Unity was writing its player/asset bundle output, likely because the temp/build drive had no free space.
cases.fixFirst: Free several GB on the build drive, clear Unity temp/cache if needed, then rebuild before investigating secondary warnings.
ignoreUntilLater: Minor UI warnings until Unity can write the build output
vrchatSpecificNotes: World build output and temp files often use C: even when the project lives elsewhere.
A disk write failure can masquerade as a generic VRChat SDK upload/build error.
recommendations: Check drive free space before large VRChat builds.
Delete old BuildPlayer temp folders and unused caches.

cases.title: CompressionLevel is ambiguous between Unity and System.IO
cases.rootCause: An editor script imported both UnityEngine and System.IO.Compression, so the unqualified CompressionLevel enum resolved ambiguously.
cases.fixFirst: Qualify the zip enum as System.IO.Compression.CompressionLevel.Optimal.
ignoreUntilLater: Snapshot feature polish before the editor assembly compiles
vrchatSpecificNotes: Editor utilities in Assets/Editor can block Unity compilation for the whole VRChat project.
Compile errors in helper tools still prevent UdonSharp workflows from stabilizing.
recommendations: Fully qualify common type names in editor scripts.
Keep backup/snapshot tools compile-clean before relying on them.

cases.title: FieldChangeCallback points at a method instead of a property
rawSignals: UdonSharp callback pattern should use a property
cases.rootCause: The synced field callback targeted a display method, but UdonSharp expects a property setter pattern for FieldChangeCallback.
cases.fixFirst: Use a synced backing field plus a public property whose setter updates the backing value and calls UpdateDisplay.
ignoreUntilLater: Text formatting before the synced callback compiles
vrchatSpecificNotes: UdonSharp supports a narrower callback pattern than ordinary C# event-style code.
Ownership should use the SDK pattern already present in the project.
recommendations: Mirror existing project UdonSharp sync patterns.
Compile after adding any UdonSynced FieldChangeCallback.

cases.title: Inspector checkbox keeps reverting because Udon public variable is serialized
cases.rootCause: The prefab/scene UdonBehaviour public-variable data still contained the old value, so the inspector appeared to restore the checkbox.
cases.fixFirst: Patch the scene/prefab serialized value or replace the checkbox with explicit NextPage and PreviousPage event methods.
ignoreUntilLater: UI layout polish before the navigation button actually calls the right action
vrchatSpecificNotes: UdonSharp public variables can be stored in both visible YAML fields and generated UdonBehaviour data.
Prefab overrides can make a changed default value look ignored.
recommendations: Avoid bool mode switches for important button direction when explicit methods are clearer.
Check both prefab and scene instances.

cases.title: ClientSim persistence callback touches a destroyed player object

sourceThread.title: Archive terminal and project snapshot work

rawSignals: ClientSim temporary player object is already gone
cases.rootCause: A persistence/player callback assumed the provided player reference was still valid and local, but ClientSim invoked it after the temporary player object had been destroyed.
cases.fixFirst: Check Utilities.IsValid(player) before reading player fields, then use Networking.LocalPlayer for local persistence reads/writes.
ignoreUntilLater: Persistence feature polish before the callback is null-safe
vrchatSpecificNotes: ClientSim can expose lifecycle timing issues that are easy to miss in normal Play mode.
VRCPlayerApi references should be validity-checked in callbacks.
recommendations: Guard every player callback before player.isLocal.
Retest with ClientSim after persistence script changes.

cases.title: Unity scene is corrupt but newer work can still be scavenged

sourceThread.title: Archive terminal and project snapshot work

cases.rootCause: The recovery snapshot had broken hierarchy/root structure, so opening it as the active scene risked more damage even though some object data still existed.
cases.fixFirst: Open a known-clean scene base, keep the corrupt scene read-only, compare by object name/fileID, then manually rebuild or scavenge selected objects.
ignoreUntilLater: Trying to recover the VRChat uploaded asset bundle as an editable Unity scene
vrchatSpecificNotes: A published VRChat world upload is not a practical editable .unity scene backup.
Scene YAML can contain useful salvage data even when the hierarchy is not load-safe.
recommendations: Validate duplicate IDs, root count, invalid parents, and invalid child refs before opening recovered scenes.
Duplicate the clean base scene before rebuilding.
Keep corrupt snapshots outside normal edit flow.

cases.title: Unity UI is modified during Canvas rebuild loop
rawSignals: Trying to remove ... from rebuild list while we are already inside a rebuild loop
cases.rootCause: Some UI object was being disabled, destroyed, or modified while Unity was already rebuilding the Canvas.
cases.fixFirst: If it affects your world UI, delay the SetActive/text/image change by one frame; otherwise treat it as a low-priority warning.
ignoreUntilLater: This warning if the build and world behavior are otherwise fine
vrchatSpecificNotes: Editor/plugin UI can emit Canvas rebuild warnings that are not world blockers.
UdonSharp can use SendCustomEventDelayedFrames for deferred UI updates.
recommendations: Search whether the named UI objects exist in your scenes before assuming it is your world.
Escalate only if the warning repeats constantly or visible UI breaks.

cases.title: Large .blend model bloats world dependencies
cases.rootCause: Unity was importing authoring data and uncompressed mesh data from a large Blender asset that contributed to upload size.
cases.fixFirst: Disable unneeded import options, enable mesh compression, or export a cleaned FBX from Blender with only the needed mesh.
ignoreUntilLater: Deleting random project assets before confirming they are scene-reachable
vrchatSpecificNotes: On-disk source size and VRChat uploaded size are different, but import settings strongly affect build size.
Sketchfab/Blender imports often carry extra cameras, lights, animation, and material slots.
recommendations: Lower non-critical textures to 1024 or 512.
Use dependency tracing to distinguish used assets from unused project clutter.
Prefer cleaned FBX exports for final world assets.

cases.title: World script cannot clone a player's actual avatar mesh
rawSignals: duplicate is actually a clone of the player
VRChat does not expose avatar meshes/materials to Udon
Can read bones/tracking but not renderers
cases.rootCause: VRChat world/Udon scripts cannot access and instantiate a player's exact avatar mesh, materials, shaders, textures, clothing, or blendshapes.
cases.fixFirst: Use a shadow/glitch humanoid proxy driven by player height, head direction, hand positions, and movement delay.
ignoreUntilLater: Trying to instantiate private avatar renderers from a world script
vrchatSpecificNotes: World scripts can use player tracking/bone data, not clone the avatar asset itself.
A mirror-render illusion can look closer to the avatar but cannot walk around as an independent clone.
recommendations: Offer VRChat-specific alternatives instead of generic clone advice.
Use synced playerId when the proxy must represent the triggering player.

cases.title: Upload fails because VRChat-side world/account state is broken
rawSignals: Build & Test succeeds but Build & Publish still fails
Upload debugger isolates the failure after Unity bundle creation
VRChat upload or API request fails even after local project checks pass
Creator had to contact VRChat support
Only VRChat could fix the account, world, blueprint, or backend state
cases.rootCause: The Unity project and local bundle build are healthy; the upload is blocked by VRChat-side world, account, blueprint, or backend state.
cases.fixFirst: Stop changing unrelated Unity assets. Preserve the failing blueprint/world/account context, collect the upload debugger output, SDK logs, blueprint ID, world ID, and account details, then contact VRChat support.
ignoreUntilLater: material and texture optimization warnings
unrelated Udon warnings if Build & Test succeeds
repeated Pipeline Manager detach attempts after debugger points to the upload API
vrchatSpecificNotes: Some upload failures are not fixable inside Unity.
A local build success plus upload API failure is a strong signal to escalate.
VRChat support may need to repair backend state tied to a world, account, or blueprint.
recommendations: Add a classifier rule for local build OK plus upload API failure plus support required.
Show this as Fix first because creators can waste hours changing assets that are not involved.
Generate a support-ticket summary from debugger evidence.
regexSignals: Build & Test succeeds but Build & Publish still fails
Upload debugger isolates the failure after Unity bundle creation
Only VRChat could fix the account, world, blueprint, or backend state

cases.title: Warnings-only Unity console should not produce Fix first blockers
cases.rootCause: Analyzer treated warnings or history as active blockers even though Unity had no current errors.
cases.fixFirst: Do not show Fix first items when current console has zero errors; rank warnings as later context only.

cases.title: Analyzer resurfaces an error that was fixed weeks ago
rawSignals: the first error was fixed 2 weeks ago
cases.rootCause: Historical corpus cases were treated as active evidence without strong current-console proof.
cases.fixFirst: Require exact or high-confidence current error evidence before showing a past case as actionable.

cases.title: Unity package manifest points to an old Error Whisperer package id
cases.rootCause: Unity manifest references a package folder/id that no longer exists after prototype rename.
cases.fixFirst: Update manifest.json and packages-lock.json to the real package id/path or restore a compatibility package.json.

cases.title: Editor menu missing because Unity loaded an empty compatibility package
rawSignals: Tools menu does not show Error Whisperer
cases.rootCause: Unity resolved the placeholder package but did not load the real editor assembly with the MenuItem.
cases.fixFirst: Point the project manifest at com.yanicloud7.error-whisperer and let Unity recompile editor scripts.

cases.title: VPM/UPM cache confusion from rebuilding without version bump
cases.rootCause: The package zip changed but kept the same version, allowing caches to serve stale package contents.
cases.fixFirst: Bump the package version whenever the distributable zip changes, then rebuild the VPM index/archive.

cases.title: Web prototype cannot load corpus JSON from raw file page
rawSignals: Run the prototype from a local web server
fetch can load the JSON corpus
cases.rootCause: Browser fetch security prevents or complicates loading local JSON from a file:// page.
cases.fixFirst: Serve the prototype from local HTTP or host it so the corpus can be fetched.

cases.title: Editor.log warnings after successful package compile are not package failures
cases.rootCause: Unity compiled the editor assembly; remaining output was warnings from unrelated scripts.
cases.fixFirst: Treat successful assembly copy plus warnings as a clean package import.

cases.title: Unity project links to a local package outside the project folder
cases.rootCause: The VRChat project depends on a local package stored in a separate prototype workspace.
cases.fixFirst: Use a stable local path for development or install through Git/VPM for testers.

cases.title: Upload debugger evidence should become a support-ticket summary
cases.rootCause: A VRChat-side upload failure is actionable only if support receives the right IDs/log evidence.
cases.fixFirst: Generate a concise support summary with blueprint ID, world ID, account, SDK log excerpt, and debugger conclusion.

cases.title: Console reader loses Unity severity and treats every line equally
rawSignals: treats every console line as equal
cases.rootCause: Raw console text without severity lets warnings trigger blocker cases.
cases.fixFirst: Prefix reflected Unity console entries with Error, Warning, or Log before matching.

cases.title: Editor tool floods a small Unity window with filler text
rawSignals: blurted out a lot of filler text
cases.rootCause: The prototype copied web-style explanation into a dense editor utility.
cases.fixFirst: Show counts and one next action first; limit recommendations and evidence.

cases.title: Door collision changes but ForceOpen/ForceClose skip animation and sound
rawSignals: only collision works but there is no animation or sound
cases.rootCause: Button-driven force methods updated state/collision without the presentation path.
cases.fixFirst: Make ForceOpen and ForceClose animate, play sound, update collision, and serialize.

cases.title: Door mesh marked Static can make moving visuals appear dead
cases.rootCause: Objects intended to move were marked Static, conflicting with runtime animation.
cases.fixFirst: Uncheck Static on moving door meshes, hinges, and animated children.

cases.title: Door frame collider assigned as moving door blocker
rawSignals: Door Colliders list contains the frame collider
do not use the frame collider
cases.rootCause: The script toggled a frame/static collider instead of a doorway blocker or door collider.
cases.fixFirst: Assign an invisible closed-door blocker or actual door mesh colliders; leave the frame collider alone.

cases.title: Door sound only sent as network event so local player hears nothing or delay
rawSignals: all works now but there is no sound
cases.rootCause: Audio playback depended on network event timing rather than immediate local feedback.
cases.fixFirst: Play locally first, then send a networked sound event with a short de-dupe guard.

cases.title: AudioSource settings make working door sounds inaudible
cases.rootCause: The code may fire but AudioSource settings can make sound inaudible.
cases.fixFirst: Check clip, mute, volume, active state, spatial blend, min distance, and max distance.

cases.title: Shared security monitor needs one synced active index
cases.rootCause: A shared monitor selector diverges if each client keeps a local selected index.
cases.fixFirst: Sync one active-view int, take ownership on click, apply locally, and request serialization.

cases.title: New UdonSharp scripts have .cs/.meta files but no Program Source assets
cases.rootCause: Unity imported C# files but UdonSharp did not generate corresponding program assets.
cases.fixFirst: Run UdonSharp compile/import repair or create missing Program Source assets through Unity editor APIs.

cases.title: Empty UdonBehaviour remains after script was wiped or missing
cases.rootCause: A scene object still has an UdonBehaviour whose script/program source is missing or empty.
cases.fixFirst: Remove the empty component or restore the missing script/program source.

cases.title: Editor repair utility needed because missing Udon assets require Unity-side execution
rawSignals: menu execution has to happen inside Unity
cases.rootCause: Some AssetDatabase/Udon repairs cannot be done reliably by external file edits alone.
cases.fixFirst: Add a scoped Unity editor repair utility that creates only the missing program assets.

cases.title: Two mirror triggers control the same effects with separate synced state
cases.rootCause: Two UdonBehaviours drive the same effects but each owns a different synced state variable.
cases.fixFirst: Centralize breach start/reset through one manager or make both triggers delegate to the same authority.

cases.title: Late joiners miss persistent effects because stages are transition-only
rawSignals: stage 2 and stage 3 were coded as transition steps
miss all the stage 1/2 persistent objects
cases.rootCause: Stage application only performs current-stage deltas, so late joiners never reconstruct earlier persistent state.
cases.fixFirst: Make ApplyStage cumulative so later stages enable all persistent state from previous stages.

cases.title: Stage progression timer can freeze when current owner leaves
cases.rootCause: Only the object owner runs delayed stage timers; if they leave, progression can stop.
cases.fixFirst: Handle ownership transfer and reschedule the remaining timer on the new owner from synced state.

cases.title: Any user-facing containment terminal can reset synced event for everyone
rawSignals: anyone is able to reset stages
cases.rootCause: The terminal requests ownership and writes global synced state, so access policy determines who can reset.
cases.fixFirst: If reset should be restricted, add role/clearance checks before ownership and serialization.

cases.title: Local Udon source changed but uploaded world may still run older generated asset
rawSignals: source changed but uploaded world still has old generated Udon
UdonSharp cache contains the new methods
cases.rootCause: Local code may be updated while the live world still behaves like an older upload.
cases.fixFirst: Check Udon compile time, generated asset contents, SDK upload log, and live build/version marker.

cases.title: Random world event timer must run on one owner, not every client
rawSignals: every client rolling its own 15 to 45 minute disaster clock
cases.rootCause: If each client schedules random events independently, shared world state desynchronizes.
cases.fixFirst: Run random event scheduling only on the authoritative owner, then serialize the chosen state.

cases.title: Automated facility event needs explicit recovery and normal reset states
cases.rootCause: Timed world events can get stuck if they do not schedule recovery and return-to-normal transitions.
cases.fixFirst: Model event duration and recovery duration explicitly, then serialize each transition back to normal.

cases.title: Quest-friendly room lighting should use emissive material swaps, not realtime lights
cases.rootCause: Realtime lighting changes are expensive or unsuitable on Quest; emissive material swaps fake them cheaply.
cases.fixFirst: Use state-driven material slots on fixture renderers and swap sharedMaterial per world state.

cases.title: TMP missing glyph warnings from emoji are noise, not the build failure
cases.rootCause: TMP font assets lack emoji glyphs, producing warnings that distract from the real build failure.
cases.fixFirst: Replace unsupported emoji with ASCII labels, but keep investigating the actual error line separately.

cases.title: Temp build folder has manifests but no real world bundle
rawSignals: actual output bundle did not finish
cases.rootCause: Unity began AssetBundle export but failed before writing the actual .vrcw bundle.
cases.fixFirst: Clear temp build output, reopen Unity, and Build & Test before attempting upload.

cases.title: Antivirus or sync tools can interfere while Unity writes the AssetBundle
rawSignals: touching the bundle while Unity writes it
cases.rootCause: External scanning/sync software can lock or modify temporary bundle output during write.
cases.fixFirst: Exclude the project/temp folder from scanning or pause sync tools during build, then retry cleanly.

cases.title: Unity/SDK cache can stay corrupted after repeated failed builds
rawSignals: Unity/SDK cache got into a bad state
cases.rootCause: Failed exports can leave stale intermediate files that poison later attempts.
cases.fixFirst: Close Unity, clear temp build output, reopen, optionally Reimport All, then Build & Test.

cases.title: Recently added asset can break AssetBundle export even when scripts compile
cases.rootCause: AssetBundle export can fail because of a corrupt/incompatible asset even when scripts compile.
cases.fixFirst: If cache cleanup fails, isolate recent assets by temporarily disabling/removing recent imports and rebuilding.

cases.title: Obsolete VRCInstantiate warning is not a current build blocker
cases.rootCause: A script uses an obsolete API that compiles with a warning, not an error.
cases.fixFirst: Leave it until blockers are resolved; later replace with the supported instantiate path if needed.

cases.title: First client sees both event paths while second sees only their own
rawSignals: first player sees both of them
cases.rootCause: Multiple local trigger paths or owners update overlapping objects, causing divergent observations.
cases.fixFirst: Map every trigger instance and referenced object; remove duplicate authorities or route through a manager.

cases.title: SDK says upload happened but runtime behavior suggests old build
rawSignals: maybe my build didnt upload even though it said it did
cases.rootCause: The creator may be testing cached or older world content despite a successful-looking publish.
cases.fixFirst: Add a visible version marker, publish again, and verify it in a fresh instance before debugging behavior.

cases.title: Network events do not preserve world state for late joiners
rawSignals: late joiner doesnt see any stages
cases.rootCause: Stage effects were driven by events current players receive, not serialized state late joiners can apply.
cases.fixFirst: Store current stage in a synced variable and apply the full snapshot in Start/OnDeserialization.

cases.title: Only triggering player hears a containment announcement
rawSignals: triggering player hears containment stabilization announcement
cases.rootCause: Announcement playback is likely local to the triggering client or owner path.
cases.fixFirst: Trigger global announcements from serialized state or a networked event to all clients, not only local feedback.

cases.title: Program asset says Manual sync but scene UdonBehaviour sync mode must also be checked
rawSignals: not just the UdonSharp program asset
cases.rootCause: Generated program asset can be correct while scene component sync mode or references are wrong.
cases.fixFirst: Inspect both generated UdonSharp asset and serialized scene UdonBehaviour component.

cases.title: Global reset behavior may be technically correct but wrong for gameplay access
rawSignals: anyone who can use the containment terminal can still reset
cases.rootCause: The sync implementation works, but access policy may allow too many players to reset globally.
cases.fixFirst: If reset should be restricted, gate terminal Interact with role/clearance before ownership/reset.

cases.title: Web and Unity prototypes can drift if they ship separate corpus copies
cases.rootCause: The web app and Unity package read different physical corpus files, so diagnoses can drift.
cases.fixFirst: Copy the validated corpus into the Unity package whenever mining changes and rebuild VPM.

cases.title: VPM repository zip still contains old package code after source edit
rawSignals: repository package would still contain the old editor code
cases.rootCause: Editing package source does not update the published VPM archive until the build script runs.
cases.fixFirst: Run the VPM repository build script after source changes and verify the zip contents.

cases.title: Editor menu missing can mean editor assembly did not compile or import
rawSignals: Unity compile error blocking editor scripts
cases.rootCause: Unity shows MenuItem entries only after editor assemblies compile successfully.
cases.fixFirst: Fix the Error Whisperer editor compile error first; Unity cannot load the latest tool UI until the package assembly compiles.

cases.title: Error triage UI should lead with action, not explanation
cases.rootCause: Creators drowning in console output need priority and next action before education.
cases.fixFirst: Display the top fix-first item and concrete next action first; put details behind compact text.

cases.title: Many console errors can collapse into a few root causes
cases.rootCause: Unity console spam repeats the same failure across assets/lines; raw count is less useful than root cause count.
cases.fixFirst: Normalize lines, collapse duplicates, and show grouped root causes instead of raw message spam.

cases.title: Quest shader advice must name VRChat-safe alternatives
cases.rootCause: Generic shader advice is less useful than VRChat-specific replacement choices.
cases.fixFirst: When detecting unsupported Quest shaders, recommend specific VRChat/Quest-compatible shader options.

cases.title: Upload readiness score is only useful when backed by reasons
cases.rootCause: A score without root-cause reasons feels arbitrary and can mislead creators.
cases.fixFirst: Compute readiness from matched findings and show the reasons that reduced the score.

cases.title: World screenshots can reveal onboarding issues that logs never show
cases.rootCause: Build logs cannot diagnose spawn clarity, sign visibility, or onboarding flow.
cases.fixFirst: Analyze screenshots separately from console errors and return UX/onboarding recommendations.

cases.title: Web MVP and VCC tool serve different testing loops
rawSignals: Make the web prototype the real MVP
both web and vcc tools are needed
if we make both we cant miss
cases.rootCause: The web app is fastest for iteration while the VCC package validates native creator workflow.
cases.fixFirst: Keep corpus/shared matching aligned while using web for MVP and Unity for workflow testing.

cases.title: Publishing prototype code is not the same as destroying enterprise potential
rawSignals: would publishing prototype code destroy it
cases.rootCause: Open alpha code can help validation, but private datasets/services should be separated if needed.
cases.fixFirst: Publish a safe alpha shell while keeping secrets, private logs, and paid backend features out.

cases.title: VPM repository requires hosted index and package zip paths to match
cases.rootCause: VCC can only install if index.json and referenced package zips are reachable at expected URLs.
cases.fixFirst: Publish index.json and packages/ together, then add the Pages index URL in VCC.

cases.title: OpenUPM distribution has stricter public package criteria
cases.rootCause: OpenUPM requires a public, valid Unity package with tags and naming conventions.
cases.fixFirst: Start with GitHub alpha and VPM; pursue OpenUPM once stable and tagged.

cases.title: Unity Asset Store is too much friction for first VRChat prototype
cases.rootCause: Asset Store review and broad Unity positioning are slower than VPM for alpha validation.
cases.fixFirst: Use GitHub alpha plus VCC/VPM first; revisit Asset Store after polish.

cases.title: Past error conversations must be mined before they can seed the corpus
rawSignals: how i send you these chats
cases.rootCause: Real creator errors are scattered across Codex conversations and need structured extraction.
cases.fixFirst: Mine thread turns for raw signals, root causes, fixes, and ignore-later guidance; validate JSON.

cases.title: A false positive is worse than saying no current blocker
cases.rootCause: A triage tool loses trust when it confidently recommends irrelevant fixed issues.
cases.fixFirst: Prefer conservative no-blocker output unless current evidence is strong; make confidence visible.
